heading: Pyth-2 page-75 wavelength items promoted with OCR notation isolated
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, page75, validated-workset, prime-factors]
body: Resolved the page-75 promotion decision under the same corpus discipline. The Quantum Prime notation item (`pyth2_p075_l5663`) remains isolated as the only page-75 OCR-corruption holdout and now lives in `pyth2_page75_issue_register.json`. The three wavelength decomposition items were promoted into the corrected-source validated lane: `pyth2_p075_l5658` as the prime-power factorization `5040 = 16 * 9 * 5 * 7`, `pyth2_p075_l5668` as the stable regrouping example `5040 = 80 * 9 * 7`, and `pyth2_p075_l5680` as the clean target `1000 = 8 * 125`. `pyth2_validated_workset.json` now has 8 stable items with counts `4 direct_validated / 4 corrected_source_validated`, and 4 holdouts total: 3 page-110 source-convention issues plus the 1 page-75 OCR notation issue. The stable Pyth-2 program/table layer was expanded accordingly: `pyth2_program_artifacts.json` now includes two prime-power wavelength cases, and `pyth2_validated_tables.json` now contains 8 stable item rows plus a `prime_power_wavelength_table` with rows for 1000 and 5040.
